items.title: Dieback disease killing mango trees is spreading in the NT

children.content: At a field day in the Northern Territory, mango growers were desperate to learn what could be done to stop the spread of mango twig tip dieback.

image.altText: Needles injected into mango trees

items.title: Almost one-third of NT children in care allegedly harmed, report finds

children.content: Almost one-third of children in out-of-home care in the Northern Territory were allegedly harmed in the last financial year, according to a new report from the Office of the Children's Commissioner.

image.altText: Children's toys, shoes, coloring pens.

items.title: Teenage boy dies after stolen car rolls on major NT highway

children.content: A teenager has died after the stolen vehicle he was inside rolled on a major Northern Territory highway in the early hours of this morning.

gallery.altText: A vehicle parked across the highway, blocking traffic access, with a logo on the side that reads: ROAD OPERATIONS

thumbnail.altText: A vehicle parked across the highway, blocking traffic access, with a logo on the side that reads: ROAD OPERATIONS

items.title: Gas workers strike as negotiations on pay deal stall

children.content: Australian workers at the Japanese gas company Inpex walked off the job this morning, and plan to strike again this evening.

image.altText: Evening / dusk shot of Darwin harbour, industrial site, with yellow lights glowing and cranes in view.

items.title: Residents still can't eat too many fish from river in PFAS-affected town

children.content: Katherine residents have welcomed a $2 billion lawsuit over PFAS contamination in the town, saying "it's about time we started holding large corporations to account".

image.altText: Anjali Palmer stands outside a medical clinic in Katherine.

items.title: Search for man missing in East Arnhem Land ends after nine days

children.content: After a nine-day operation covering more than 1,300 square kilometres of land, the search for a 31-year-old man in East Arnhem Land has been stood down.

image.altText: A Northern Territory police vehicle in Katherine.

items.title: NT WorkSafe charges health department over hospital aerosol can death

children.content: WorkSafe charges Northern Territory Health with failing to comply with health and safety duties over the death of a mental health in-patient at Royal Darwin Hospital in 2022.

image.altText: Emergency department of hospital with sunset behind it

items.title: Historic journey along Overland Telegraph Line captures the imagination

children.content: If you saw a car in the central Australian outback you wouldn't expect it to be a vintage 1925 Studebaker ... let alone two of them, driven by two men called Brenton.

gallery.altText: Two men in blue shirts smile for a selfie inside a vintage Studebaker. The man on the left wears a hat and sunnies.

thumbnail.altText: Two men in blue shirts smile for a selfie inside a vintage Studebaker. The man on the left wears a hat and sunnies.

items.title: US Navy interested in Darwin ship lift, NT minister says

children.content: Northern Territory Infrastructure Minister Bill Yan says the United States Navy has expressed interest in using the controversial Darwin ship lift, which is expected to be operational next year.

image.altText: Construction site with several cranes on a harbour

items.title: Decades-old trees in Darwin finally flower for the first, and only, time

children.content: About 20 Gebang palms have flowered in Darwin in a rare spectacle, reaffirming the local council's plans to foster a new generation of the trees.

image.altText: A close-up shot of a Gebang palm tree in flower, with small yellow tufts growing outward.

items.title: 'Even sleep is expensive': The challenge for a people forced to live in ruins

children.content: It's been seven months since the ceasefire in Gaza, but reconstruction has barely begun, water remains contaminated and minimal aid is getting into the strip. This is what life is like for its residents.

gallery.altText: A man brushes another person's hair in a barber's chair.
Damaged buildings in the background and a large number of tents in the foreground in Gaza City.
Two women wearing hijabs crouching down on the ground and washing clothes in buckets.

thumbnail.altText: A man brushes another person's hair in a barber's chair.
Damaged buildings in the background and a large number of tents in the foreground in Gaza City.
Two women wearing hijabs crouching down on the ground and washing clothes in buckets.

items.title: Starmer condemns handcuffed teen's death as police response sparks protests

children.content: The British prime minister said he "felt sick" while watching video released by police of 18-year-old Henry Nowak, who was handcuffed while he lay dying from stab wounds after his killer falsely alleged a racist attack.

image.altText: The heads and hands of protesters, including one holding a white sign with a picture of a Caucasian teen.

items.title: Trump drops $2.5b 'weaponization' fund plans amid corruption allegations

children.content: The US president has abandoned plans for a $2.51 billion "anti-weaponization" fund for ‌victims of alleged government "weaponization", according to the acting attorney-general.

image.altText: Donald Trump speaking during a cabinet meeting at the White House.

items.title: Trump names inexperienced ally as intelligence director

children.content: Bill Pulte, who is known for publicly attacking the US president's political enemies, would also retain his existing jobs overseeing federal housing and mortgage policies.

image.altText: A man in a suit smiles.

items.title: Marles says Australia wanted three second-hand subs all along

children.content: The defence minister says the new AUKUS deal that will see Australia buy three second-hand Virginia-class submarines from the US is what the government had wanted all along. Follow live.

image.altText: Richard Marles speaks during an AUKUS Defence ministers' press huddle

items.title: ASX trades higher as AI drives Wall Street to record highs, oil rises on US-Iran uncertainty

children.content: Oil prices jump to a one-week high as the US and Iran provide conflicting updates on their peace talks. The Australian share market starts its day slightly higher after a surge in AI stocks pushed Wall Street to fresh record highs. Follow live.

image.altText: Giant screen on the wall of the ASX, with large green numbers, showing the price of shares which have risen.

items.title: Why Hugh Jackman's brother cried on his first day as a prison-school teacher

children.content: After Ralph Jackman retrained as a teacher, his first job was in a youth detention centre. His new memoir reveals how a broken system is failing vulnerable kids.

image.altText: Three teenage boys sitting at desks in a classroom, facing the teacher, who is standing in front of a whiteboard.

items.title: Enormous ship en route to pick up world's largest electric ferry

children.content: The 217-metre-long heavy-lift vessel Black Marlin will deliver a Tasmanian shipbuilder's new electric ferry to South America, after the previous transporter became caught up in the Strait of Hormuz closure.

gallery.altText: A gigantic ship in the middle of the ocean

thumbnail.altText: A gigantic ship in the middle of the ocean

items.title: Former Canberra Liberals leader leaves party citing 'threat of physical assault'

children.content: Former Canberra Liberals leader Leanne Castley says she has experienced "bullying, intimidation, lies and a threat of physical assault" in the past 18 months.

image.altText: A politician in a pink jacket stands in front of microphones.

items.title: Renegades BBL team to be sold as new Victorian franchise established

children.content: The Melbourne Renegades BBL licence is set to be sold to a private investor, with Cricket Victoria to establish a new team, marking the end of the Stars as well.

image.altText: a batter dressed in green hits the ball, while a keeper in red is behind him at the stumps

items.title: This rapper was having panic attacks on stage, until he transitioned

children.content: Poet, rapper, musician, playwright and novelist Kae Tempest is a prodigious polymath. It wasn't until he affirmed his gender that he connected with his true self.

image.altText: Close-up of Kae Tempest looking down at sunset, with a city skyline in silhouette behind them.

items.title: Quiz: Hold on to your hat, we're off on a tour of the globe

children.content: The famous US road trip along Route 66 links which two cities? And which country eats the most garlic per person?

image.altText: A statue of a woman with a city in the background and a full moon above her head

items.title: Deadly heatwave 'tip of the iceberg' in India as UN sends El Niño warning

children.content: India's deadly heatwave comes as the UN warns the world to prepare for a moderate to strong El Niño this year.

gallery.altText: A man puts his head under a running outdoor tap.
Two girls walk along a dry, cracked ground with a scarf over their heads
A man pours water into the hand of another man with a rickshaw in the background.

thumbnail.altText: A man puts his head under a running outdoor tap.
Two girls walk along a dry, cracked ground with a scarf over their heads
A man pours water into the hand of another man with a rickshaw in the background.

items.title: These companies have controversial pasts. Google AI didn't tell us

children.content: We searched Google for information on some of the world's biggest tobacco companies. The results raised questions about how much of the AI overview was coming from the brands themselves.

image.altText: A hand holding a phone where the user has googled tobacco company Philip Morris.

items.title: Driver found 'eating Maccas' in car that crashed into Sydney fountain

children.content: The CBD fountain sustained minimal damage after a French national allegedly drove into it early this morning.

image.altText: a white car on a truck's tray

items.title: Plenty of jobs but no housing: Could 'flat-pack' homes help save this town?

children.content: A Queensland outback town pins its hopes of solving a local housing crisis on "flat-pack" homes as the high cost of building risks paralysing the community.

image.altText: white house without a roof sits on block of land in the outback

items.title: Government says it preferred second-hand submarines

children.content: Australia and the United States' initial positions about delivering a new submarine under AUKUS differed, Senate estimates has revealed.

image.altText: Part of the hull and control room of a submarine that has surfaced from underwater.

items.title: Major Russian attack on Ukraine leaves at least 22 dead and dozens injured

children.content: Moscow targeted Kyiv and other cities, killing multiple people, damaging buildings and leaving wounded trapped under rubble, local authorities say.

image.altText: The silhouette of a man taking a photo on an empty road underneath a fiery red skyline clouded by black smoke.

items.title: Black market adapts after Qld's illicit tobacco and vape shop crackdown

children.content: Queensland's crackdown has reduced visible illicit tobacco sales, according to a public health researcher, but the black market has adapted, prompting calls for the tobacco excise to be cut.

image.altText: Close of a person lighting a cigarette

items.title: Semaglutide is used for weight loss but may benefit kidneys and heart

children.content: Research shows semaglutide — commonly sold as Ozempic or Wegovy — can improve kidney function and survival rates for people with some chronic diseases, even if they have heart issues.

gallery.altText: Two boxes of medication side by side, labelled Ozempic and Wegovy

thumbnail.altText: Two boxes of medication side by side, labelled Ozempic and Wegovy

items.title: Emotional Ukrainian star not accepting silence from Russian players

children.content: Ukrainian star Marta Kostyuk says it is unacceptable for Russians to stay silent on the war, especially after Daria Kasatkina's defection to Australia.

image.altText: Marta Kostyuk wipes away a tear during an on-court interview at the French Open.

items.title: Green top-scores and Ellis stands tall as Australia levels ODI series

children.content: Nathan Ellis shines with the ball, while Cameron Green tops the run-scoring for Australia in a 41-run ODI victory over Pakistan in Lahore.

image.altText: Cameron Green cuts a cricket ball away as Pakistan's wicketkeeper looks on.

items.title: Premier tells member to take complaint to body which doesn't exist yet

children.content: Jeremy Rockliff won't investigate how an email containing the health-related information of a politician was leaked to the media and suggests she could take up the matter with a complaints body that doesn't exist yet.

image.altText: Man looks out to the distance, leaning on a green chair

items.title: Medical clinics closing down 'like dominoes' with new bulk-billing model

children.content: Kalbarri residents will be forced to travel more than 100 kilometres to visit a doctor when the regional WA town's only GP clinic closes due to financial pressure.

image.altText: Image of Monica wearing a t shirt and her hair tied back. She stands in front of the ocean and looks concerned at camera.

items.title: Poor advertising, confused voters and underfunded candidates: SA Voice's problems revealed

children.content: New documents suggest South Australia's inaugural Voice to Parliament election was hamstrung by advertising failures that left First Nations voters confused or unaware an election was taking place.

gallery.altText: Scott Wilson looks at the camera with a serious look on his face. He has grey hair, a full beard, and is wearing a black jacket.

thumbnail.altText: Scott Wilson looks at the camera with a serious look on his face. He has grey hair, a full beard, and is wearing a black jacket.
